confirmed.title: core.md lifecycle hook signatures wrong: before* hooks return array, beforeCreate has no $record
confirmed.suggested_fix: Update the hook block to `beforeCreate(array $data): array`, `beforeUpdate(Model $record, array $data): array`, `beforeSave(Model $record, array $data): array` (keep after* as `: void`). Apply the same fix in the pt-BR and es copies.
confirmed.detail: The reference documents the Resource lifecycle hooks as `beforeCreate(Model $record, array $data): void`, `beforeUpdate(Model $record, array $data): void`, `beforeSave(Model $record, array $data): void`. The actual source in packages/core/src/Resources/Resource.php disagrees: line 264 `protected function beforeCreate(array $data): array` (NO $record param, returns array), line 276 `protected function beforeUpdate(Model $record, array $data): array` (returns array, not void), line 288 `protected function beforeSave(Model $record, array $data): array` (returns array, not void). These hooks mutate-and-return the data array; documenting them as `: void` is actively misleading (an override returning void would break the create/update pipeline). The after* hooks and orchestrators (runCreate/runUpdate/runDelete) are correct. Same wrong block exists in apps/docs/pt-BR/reference/php/core.md:27 and apps/docs/es/reference/php/core.md:27.
confirmed.why_real: Verified both sides on disk. Doc at apps/docs/reference/php/core.md:27 documents the before* lifecycle hooks as: beforeCreate(Model $record, array $data): void, beforeUpdate(Model $record, array $data): void, beforeSave(Model $record, array $data): void. The actual source packages/core/src/Resources/Resource.php contradicts this: line 264 `protected function beforeCreate(array $data): array` (no $record param, returns array), line 276 `protected function beforeUpdate(Model $record, array $data): array` (returns array), line 288 `protected function beforeSave(Model $record, array $data): array` (returns array). The mutate-and-return contract is corroborated by the runCreate orchestrator docblock describing the beforeSave→beforeCreate→persist pipeline. Documenting `: void` would lead an overrider to drop the return value, breaking the create/update data flow — objectively false and misleading, not a style nit. The doc is in-scope reference (not PLANNING/tickets/CHANGELOG/generated) and not present in docs-fixed-ledger.json.

confirmed.title: table reference claims '9 column types' but 12 exist on disk
confirmed.suggested_fix: Change count to 12 and add SelectColumn, TextInputColumn, ToggleColumn rows to the column table; update the pt-BR/es/SKILL.md copies to match.
confirmed.detail: Header states 'Fluent builder, 9 column types, 6 filter types'. packages/table/src/Columns/ contains 12 concrete Column subclasses: BadgeColumn, BooleanColumn, ComputedColumn, DateColumn, IconColumn, ImageColumn, NumberColumn, RelationshipColumn, SelectColumn, TextColumn, TextInputColumn, ToggleColumn. The doc's column table (lines 30-39) only lists 9 and omits SelectColumn, TextInputColumn, ToggleColumn (all concrete `final class ... extends Column`, e.g. ToggleColumn.php is a fully-featured editable toggle cell). Same '9 column types' claim in apps/docs/pt-BR/reference/php/table.md:3 and apps/docs/es/reference/php/table.md:3 ('9 tipos de columna'), and in packages/table/SKILL.md:16 ('9 Column types').
confirmed.why_real: Verified both sides on disk. apps/docs/reference/php/table.md:3 header states "Fluent builder, 9 column types, 6 filter types". The column reference table (lines 28-39) lists exactly 9 classes: TextColumn, BadgeColumn, BooleanColumn, DateColumn, NumberColumn, IconColumn, ImageColumn, RelationshipColumn, ComputedColumn. However packages/table/src/Columns/ contains 12 concrete `final class ... extends Column` subclasses — the same 9 PLUS SelectColumn (line 15: `final class SelectColumn extends Column`), TextInputColumn (line 16: `final class TextInputColumn extends Column`), and ToggleColumn (line 15: `final class ToggleColumn extends Column`). These three are fully-featured editable cell columns (the project's own SKILL.md line 26 documents them as "3 editable column types"). So "9 column types" is an objectively false undercount and the table omits 3 real, shipped column types. The doc is in-scope (apps/docs, not PLANNING/tickets/CHANGELOG/generated), the claim is concrete and checkable, and the ledger is empty []. Confidence high.

confirmed.title: table reference claims '6 filter types' but 8 exist on disk
confirmed.suggested_fix: Change count to 8 and add QueryBuilderFilter and TrashedFilter rows; update the pt-BR/es/SKILL.md copies to match.
confirmed.detail: Header states '6 filter types'. packages/table/src/Filters/ contains 8 concrete Filter subclasses: DateRangeFilter, MultiSelectFilter, QueryBuilderFilter, ScopeFilter, SelectFilter, TernaryFilter, TextFilter, TrashedFilter (verified each is `final class ... extends Filter/SelectFilter`). The doc's filter table (lines 47-54) lists only 6, omitting QueryBuilderFilter and TrashedFilter. Same '6 filter types' claim in apps/docs/pt-BR/reference/php/table.md:3, apps/docs/es/reference/php/table.md:3 ('6 tipos de filtro'), and packages/table/SKILL.md:18 ('6 Filter types').
confirmed.why_real: Confirmed both sides on disk. apps/docs/reference/php/table.md:3 states 'Namespace Arqel\Table\. Fluent builder, 9 column types, 6 filter types, query builder.' and the Filters table (lines 47-53) lists exactly 6: SelectFilter, MultiSelectFilter, DateRangeFilter, TextFilter, TernaryFilter, ScopeFilter. packages/table/src/Filters/ on disk contains 8 concrete filter source files, and I verified each disputed one is a real concrete subclass: 'final class QueryBuilderFilter extends Filter' and 'final class TrashedFilter extends Filter' (TrashedFilter documented as 'First-class soft-delete (trashed) filter'). Both are omitted from the doc and from the count. The '6 filter types' count is objectively false vs 8 on disk. File is in-scope (apps/docs/, not PLANNING/tickets/CHANGELOG/generated), ledger (reports/docs-fixed-ledger.json) is empty []. Same false count appears in pt-BR/es mirrors and packages/table/SKILL.md per the finding, but the cited primary file alone confirms the contradiction. Note: QueryBuilderFilter arguably maps to the 'query builder' phrase at line 3, but TrashedFilter is an unambiguous omitted filter type, so the count is wrong regardless.

confirmed.title: core.md lists Resource::actions() as an existing method, but it is not defined
confirmed.suggested_fix: Remove the actions() row from the Resource method table (and the pt-BR/es copies), or relabel it clearly as not-yet-implemented on the base class, matching the HasActions marker-interface reality.
confirmed.detail: The Resource method table documents `actions()` → `array<Action>` with 'Optional. Empty default'. No such method exists on packages/core/src/Resources/Resource.php (public methods are: effectiveFields, fields, form, getLabel, getModel, getNavigationGroup/Icon/Sort, getPluralLabel, getSlug, indexQuery, recordSubtitle, recordTitle, runCreate/Update/Delete, table). Resource implements the marker interface HasActions, but packages/core/src/Contracts/HasActions.php is an empty marker whose own docblock states 'The concrete actions() and tableActions() methods will be added once arqel-dev/actions and arqel-dev/table ship'. So a user calling/overriding actions() per this table has no base method and no 'empty default'.
confirmed.why_real: Verified both sides on disk. apps/docs/reference/php/core.md:20 lists `actions()` → `array<Action>` as 'Optional. Empty default', presenting it as an existing overridable Resource method with a base default. Reality: packages/core/src/Resources/Resource.php defines no actions() method (grep for 'function actions' in Resources/ returns nothing; public methods are getModel/getSlug/getLabel/getPluralLabel/getNavigation*/fields/indexQuery/table/form/effectiveFields/recordTitle/recordSubtitle/runCreate/runUpdate/runDelete plus protected lifecycle hooks). Resource implements HasActions, but packages/core/src/Contracts/HasActions.php is an empty marker interface (`interface HasActions {}`) whose docblock states the concrete actions()/tableActions() methods 'will be added once arqel-dev/actions and arqel-dev/table ship'. Thus there is no base method and no 'empty default'; the doc claim is objectively false/misleading. In-scope (apps/docs reference, not PLANNING/tickets/CHANGELOG/generated). Ledger (reports/docs-fixed-ledger.json) is empty [], so not a duplicate.

confirmed.title: publishing-setup.md package counts are stale/contradictory vs 20 PHP + 18 JS on disk
confirmed.suggested_fix: Reconcile the counts to the current 20 PHP + 18 JS packages (release.yml is the source of truth for the split list), and drop or clearly mark the v0.1.0-era '15 PHP / 4 JS' snapshot as historical.
confirmed.detail: The doc cites several package counts that no longer match the repo: line 27 'o split SHA é computado para todos os 19 pacotes ... lista os 16 pacotes npm'; line 170/449 'os 18 packages'/'18 sub-repos'; line 453 'todos os 15 PHP packages'; line 454 '4 JS packages'. Actual: packages/ has 20 PHP composer packages and the release workflow (.github/workflows/release.yml lines 79-98) splits all 20; packages-js/ has 18 npm packages. The same doc (line 10) states the last release was v0.13.0 with all packages, which contradicts the v0.1.0 '15 PHP / 4 JS' bootstrap snapshot still embedded lower in the file.
confirmed.why_real:
Confirmed both sides on disk. Doc docs/publishing-setup.md is in-scope (not PLANNING/docs-tickets/superpowers/CHANGELOG/generated). Ledger reports/docs-fixed-ledger.json is empty ([]), so not a duplicate.

The cited package counts are objectively false vs current reality:
- Line 27: "o split SHA é computado para todos os 19 pacotes ... lista os 16 pacotes npm". Actual: release.yml split-php matrix (lines 79-98) enumerates exactly 20 PHP packages, and packages/ on disk has 20 dirs each with composer.json. packages-js/ has 18 dirs each with package.json. So 19 PHP and 16 npm are both wrong (should be 20 and 18).
- Line 449: "push para os 18 sub-repos" with tag v0.1.0 — actual 20 PHP sub-repos in the matrix.
- Line 453: "todos os 15 PHP packages a 0.1.0" — actual 20.
- Line 454: "4 JS packages a 0.1.0" — actual 18.

These are checkable numeric claims, not style/wording nits, and not forward-looking PLANNING statements: the doc's own header (line 7 "Estado atual do repo (atualizado 2026-06-08)", line 10 "Última release publicada v0.13.0, todos a 0.13.0") frames the file as describing the live repo, while the embedded counts still reflect a stale 15-PHP/4-JS bootstrap snapshot. The split matrix and the package directories are concrete artifacts present right now that contradict every cited number. The internal contradiction (line 10 says all packages shipped at v0.13.0; lines 453-454 still say 15 PHP / 4 JS) further confirms drift.

Note: the finding's "actual 20 PHP" is corroborated; the finding also correctly states packages-js has 18 npm packages. All cited line numbers match the file read.

Reality contradicts both: `ls apps/demo/scripts/` returns "No such file or directory"; `find` for recreate.sh anywhere in the repo returns nothing; and `git ls-files apps/demo` returns 83 tracked files (app/Arqel/Resources/PostResource.php, composer.json, Models, providers, resources, tests, etc.) proving apps/demo IS committed versioned scaffolding. The cited broken-ref and the "recreated from scratch / no versioned scaffolding" premise are both objectively false. Doc is in-scope (apps/docs, not PLANNING/tickets/CHANGELOG/generated). Ledger (reports/docs-fixed-ledger.json) is empty [], so not a duplicate.

On disk, packages/ contains: actions, ai, arqel, audit, auth, cli, core, export, fields, fields-advanced, form, marketplace, mcp, nav, realtime, table, tenant, versioning, widgets, workflow. 'panel' (packages/panel) and 'testing' (packages/testing) do NOT exist — they are phantom entries the split script would push to nonexistent arqel-dev/panel and arqel-dev/testing repos. The array also omits real PHP packages (ai, realtime, versioning, workflow, marketplace, arqel, cli). PACKAGES_JS likewise omits most of packages-js/ (a11y, ai, auth, cli-ink, devtools-extension, fields-advanced, fields-js, i18n, mcp-server, realtime, realtime-collab, theme, versioning, workflow). Confidence reduced from high to med only because the snippet sits in a 'script to be created' code block, giving mild forward-looking framing — but the package enumeration itself is a concrete factual list that contradicts disk reality.
